name: Hiking along the banks of the Jalón River – running round trip from Arcos de Jalón
name_original: Somaén por el margen del río Jalón – running ida y vuelta desde Arcos de Jalón
region_intro: Jogging routes around Arcos de Jalón are set within the diverse landscapes of Soria, where the Jalón River valley provides a central natural feature. The region features varied terrain, from fertile plains along the river to significant elevation changes in the Judes highlands, offering options for different running preferences. Unique ecosystems like the Judes Lagoon, a karst wetland, and the Sabinares del Jalón contribute to the area's natural appeal. This combination of riverine environments and varied topography creates a compelling setting for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Arcos De Jalón The most popular jogging route is Hiking along the banks of the Jalón River – running round trip from Arcos de Jalón, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the scenic Jalón River, offering a tranquil running experience. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Chércoles, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.7 km) path. This route provides a balanced run through the local countryside, featuring gentle ascents and descents. Local runners also love the Running loop from Arcos de Jalón, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through the immediate surroundings of the town, often completed in about 23 minutes. Jogging around Arcos de Jalón is defined by riverine landscapes, varied terrain with elevation changes, and unique natural reserves like the Judes Lagoon. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate runs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 15 runners have used komoot to explore Arcos de Jalón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Arcos de Jalón?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;5 dedicated running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Arcos de Jalón featured in this guide, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Arcos de Jalón?
A: Yes, for those looking for an easier run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Arcos de Jalón&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 3.6 km trail leads through the immediate surroundings of the town and is classified as easy.

Q: Can I find running trails along the Jalón River?
A: Absolutely. The Jalón River is a central natural feature of the region. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064088605/hiking-along-the-banks-of-the-jalon-river-running-round-trip-from-arcos-de-jalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking along the banks of the Jalón River – running round trip from Arcos de Jalón&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a tranquil running experience along its scenic banks.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Chércoles&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.7 km path that provides a balanced run through the local countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church loop from Anguita&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Arcos de Jalón?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from fertile plains along the Jalón River to areas with significant elevation changes, such as the Judes highlands. You can find both relatively flat riverside runs and more challenging routes with gentle ascents and descents, catering to various preferences.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest I can see while jogging?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You might encounter historical sights like the 14th-century Arcos Castle or the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción. Additionally, you can explore nearby castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6022001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6020762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somaén Castle&lt;/a&gt;, adding a cultural dimension to your run.

Q: Are there any waterfalls near the running routes in Arcos de Jalón?
A: While not directly on the running routes listed, the broader region features beautiful waterfalls that could be explored separately. Notable examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar de Mesa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5999948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Chorroneras of Chaorna&lt;/a&gt;, which are highlights in the surrounding area.

Q: What makes Arcos de Jalón a unique place for running?
A: Arcos de Jalón stands out due to its combination of riverine landscapes along the Jalón River, varied terrain with elevation changes, and unique natural reserves like the Judes Lagoon, a karst wetland. This diverse environment provides a rich and engaging setting for runners.

Q: What is the longest running route available in this guide?
A: The longest running route featured is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064088605/hiking-along-the-banks-of-the-jalon-river-running-round-trip-from-arcos-de-jalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking along the banks of the Jalón River – running round trip from Arcos de Jalón&lt;/a&gt;, covering approximately 11.4 km (7.1 miles).

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views or natural features?
A: Many routes offer scenic views, particularly those along the Jalón River, which provide picturesque and tranquil environments. The region's varied topography also means you can find trails with significant elevation changes, potentially offering broader vistas. The unique Judes Lagoon, a karst wetland, is another distinct natural point of interest in the area.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Arcos de Jalón?
A: The running trails around Arcos de Jalón are appreciated by the komoot community for their varied terrain and scenic beauty. Many runners enjoy the tranquil experience along the Jalón River and the opportunities to explore the local countryside, with routes catering to different ability levels.
tips: It is in a state of consolidated ruin. Some canvases of the wall and the solid keep, with a height of 16 meters, have been preserved. It was built in masonry with rows (verdugadas) of brick in the purest Moorish style, with the corners of the keep in masonry. The castle has a triangular floor plan, with the access door on the east wall, next to the Keep, and on it appear the coats of arms of the Albornoz and Luna family.
The Camino del Cid in Somaén is a fascinating historical and cultural route. Somaén, located between Medinaceli and Arcos de Jalón, is a strategic point in the Jalón River gorge.

Somaén is a picturesque town in the province of Soria, known for its charming cobblestone streets and stunning natural surroundings. It is a perfect place for a quiet excursion and enjoy the natural and architectural beauty of the town.

waypoints.name: Camino del Cid near Arcos de Jalón
